5. Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the municipality/city of last known address, Proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect the receivable and demand to liquidate the cash advances and fund transfers, such as but not limited to copies of served or returned demand letters,
6. Certification by Legal Officer of the entity of no pending case relative to the account, certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate the claim;
7. Other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter of request; and
When accounts are non-moving for ten (10) years or more, the Head of Accounting Unit shall prepare the following sched Schedule of Dormant Receivables, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Intra/Inter-Agency Fund Transfers; and Schedule of Other Dormant Accounts for monitoring purposes, to be submitted to the Audit Team Leader (ATL)/Supervising Auditor (SA) in compliance with COA Circular No. 2023-008, and adopt the following procedures:
a. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of all dormant accounts;
b. Secure/collate all available documents relative to the said accounts, such as books of accounts, records, financial statements, ledgers, schedules, vouchers/documents and all other documents;
c. Review, analyze and determine the existence and validity of the accounts;
d. Reconcile the account with other related accounts in the trial balance;
e. Ensure fairness in the presentation of accounts in the financial statements, taking into consideration the provisions of applicable IPSAS or PFRS on specific accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entry/.
f. If the review/analysis of the account/s is not possible due to the absence of records and documents or when original documents are lost or destroyed, or cannot be produced, in consonance with COA Circular No. 93-404 dated October 18, 1993.

i..Prepare letters, signed by the Head of Agency (HoA), addressed to each concerned accountable officer (AO)/personnel (i.e. the officer/s or employee/s responsible for handling that particular account) demanding the AO to execute a certificate of justification as to why the supporting documents are not available in his/her office or in any other office. He/she shall also include a narration/documentation of all the efforts exerted by Management to locate such documents. In case of retirement, death or separation from service of AO, the current AO shall execute the required certificate of justification. Refer to Annex 5 for the pro-forma certificate of justification.

ii. Henceforth, clearance must not be issued to AOs unless the appropriate documentation on transfer of accountability to the new accountable official and/or request for relief from accountability for the loss or damaged documents have been filed with the Commission.

iii. Prepare a list of available records relative to the dormant account and indicate the extent of validation made thereof All documents must be attached to the list. Include a detailed list of the lost documents or records; and

iv. Prepare and submit a detailed report to the HoA together with all the documents in Items 7 l.f.i and 7.l.f.iii, recommending the conduct of an investigation to determine the cause/s of the loss of documents and pinpoint the official/s and employee/s liable for such loss.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management concurred to direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the implementing departments to confirm whether the projects charged to particular funds were actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds.
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall likewise determine if the unexpended balance accrue to the National Government or the Municipality in case of counterpart funds. If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrued to the funds, the Municipality shall take the following actions:
For funds accruing to NGAs, return immediately the unexpended balance to SA. If the SA cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury; or
For counterpart funds, the Local Finance Committee shall conduct the necessary evaluation and thereafter give recommendation on the proper disposition of the funds in relation to the priority projects of the Municipality.
Lastly, initiate the immediate closure of the bank accounts once the balance is returned to the SA.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts in an on-line we-access of the Land Bank of the Philippines and grant authority to the Municipal Accountant to access and view the balances on-line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee as well as the Technical Working Group to ensure that all procurements of Infrastructure are included in the Annual Procurement Plan and all implementation thereof are conducted thru public bidding. In the event that implementation by administration is more practicable and advantageous, it shall ensure full compliance with the conditions provided under GPPB Resolution No. 20-2020; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to accomplish and submit the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and Certificates of Completion for every infrastructure project implemented by administration.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Focal person to:
Refrain from monthly transferring an amount to the special trust fund for LDRRMF except during year-end;
Submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2021 as well as the Disbursement Vouchers and other supporting documents under the LDRRMF account; and
Strictly observe the aforementioned law and circular.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the subsidiary ledger of the accounts Due to Other Funds and Loans Payable – Domestic;
Submit the latest copy of amortization schedule and Billing Statement/Statement of Account from LBP; and
Strictly observe the provisions of abovementioned laws.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Prepare and submit the RCD along with the duplicate copy of issued ORs and the RAAF for CY 2021;
Request for additional personnel to facilitate the preparation of the required reports; and
Strictly abide by the aforementioned rules.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure strict compliance with the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before affixing his/her signature to the Disbursement Vouchers; and
Refrain from using RER for expenses exceeding above P1,000 and/or purchase supplies and materials from entities issuing ORs/Invoice.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Heads, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), and the other personnel involved in the preparation of the Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders to ensure that the same are adequately accomplished as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2002 – 003 and COA Circular No 96-010. Moreover, we advised the Municipal Accountant to verify the completeness of the documents before affixing his/her signature to the Disbursement Vouchers.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the submission of the Purchase Orders to the Auditor’s Office within five (5) days after conformity by the supplier.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant to:
Assess the situations/problems/lapses regarding the delayed submission of reports and documents and adopt an effective and efficient method in order to fasten the submission thereof; and
Require the accountable officers/bookkeepers involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts to submit the report and disbursement record with all their paid vouchers and documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period in compliance with provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Chapter 4 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant to:
Update the list of employees in the Membership Contribution Remittance Form (MCRF);
Undertake the necessary adjustment to properly establish the amount of Due to PAGIBIG in the financial statements and reports; and
Strictly abide by the aforementioned law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enroll the bank accounts in We-Access offered by the LBP or request snapshots as alternative to bank statements to facilitate timely preparation of BRS. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the timeline of submission of BRS  to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Accountant ensure that purchases are accounted under Inventories. Henceforth, the receipts and issuances shall be recorded by the Accounting Office in the Supplies Ledger Cards and by the General Services Office in the Stock Cards pursuantto Sections 182 and 187 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, and reconcile their records with the report on the physical count.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee for the conduct semi-annual physical count of unissued supplies and materials pursuant to Section 194 of the same Manual.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count which the Inventory Committee had started in November, 2021; henceforth, require them to conduct regularly annual physical count of property, plant and equipment that will include land, land improvements, buildings, other structures and construction in progress.
We recommended that the General Services Office and the Accounting Office maintain PC and PPELC, respectively, and coordinate with each other to reconcile their records with the physical inventory report.
We recommended that the Accountant recognize accounts payables only when goods/services have been delivered/rendered and accepted.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that Prior Period Adjustments are properly disclosed in the financial statements, the financial statement of the preceding year is restated, and the financial Statements are free from material errors.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Audit Team detail of the prior period errors.
We recommended that the Treasurer and the Accountant submit the above-mentioned reports, and the transaction documents within the prescribed period and ensure that they are complete.

We recommended to the Municipal Administrator:
a. To exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles by verifying the correctness and completeness of Driver’s Trip Tickets
b. To submit Monthly Fuel Consumption Reports and Monthly Report of Travels; and,
c. To ensure proper markings of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality of Tagoloan.
Moreover, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant who is in charge of the internal audit services under Article 122 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code, to establish and monitor internal control over fuel consumption. The aforementioned reports can be utilized in reviewing and regulating the fuel consumption of the Local Government Unit.
We recommended that the COMPRAISE adhere to the criteria on the grant of monetary award.
We recommended that management stop granting benefits to job order employees.
We recommended that the Local School Board conform with the provisions of DBM, DepEd and DILG  JC no. 01 s 2017 on the utilization of SEF.

We recommended the following:
a. 	For the Local Chief Executive to ensure that the development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan are feasible and can be implemented within the year; and
b. 	For the concerned officials to provide explanation on the delay in the perfection of Contract Agreement up to receipt of Notice to Proceed for the installation of streetlights along Sta. Cruz-Mohon Road.

For the Construction of Level III, Water System in Barangay Natumolan, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee:
a. 	Be updated on the issuances of the Government Procurement Policy Board and produce legal basis in declaring EJ's Engineering Services and Construction Supply eligible for the project even though it did not meet the eligibility requirements stipulated in the Bid Data Sheet; and,
b. 	Demand from the contractor Certificates of Registration of all vehicles listed in the Equipment Schedule which should be valid during bidding process.
We also recommended that the procuring entity provide the derivation of completion date of December 10, 2021.
For the Construction of 6 units isolation/quarantine facilities for LSI and ROF in Barangay Natumolan, we recommended:
a. 	That the head of the Procuring Entity  investigate the similarity of the bid with the ABC, including errors; and,
b. 	That the head of the Procuring Entity provide justification for resorting to negotiated procurement-emergency cases but lacking the pressure to start and complete the project at shortest time.
Also, we recommended that the Engineering Office to use the date of receipt of Notice to Proceed as start date of the contractor to commence execution of the works and adopt calendar days for contract duration.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer submit duly signed distribution list showing receipt by indigent and affected families of specific number of bags of rice provided to them and documents that would prove that the recipients were under community quarantine and are indigent and affected.

We recommended that management require:
The Municipal Health Office to provide certified summary list of patients and duration of their confinement per isolation area and proof of receipt of meals by them; proof of receipt of  three meals by other personnel of the Municipal Office who were involved and authorized for the mentioned activity; and
b.    The General Service Unit  to submit Inspection Acceptance Report for each date of delivery with the quantity, type of meals and time (breakfast, lunch, dinner) indicated thereof .
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accounting Officeto ensure that taxes withheld are remitted promptly and fully.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that the contributions and deductions/amount withheld are remitted promptly and fully and to periodically examine and monitor the balance; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate:
to submit copies of cancelled or paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos, and other documents necessary to facilitate the timely verification of the bank reconciliation statements.
to attend seminars or training related to preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and other reports related to the function of accountant.
to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in adherence to COA Circular No. 96-011. Also require her to get the monthly bank statements from the depository bank promptly and regularly
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e- access in the Land Bank of the Philippines and grant authority to the Municipal Accountant to access and view the balances on line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 Therefore, we strongly recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an
extensive and thorough physical count of the road network
system under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare the reports
thereon in accordance with templates provided in COA Circular
2015-008;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road
network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components using
the prescribed template; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

2.6 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously
post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and
property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep
and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the
Manual;
b) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the
RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each
year.
c) That upon completion of the physical count, mandate the General
Services Office and Accounting Office to undertake collaborative
effort to maintain, update, and reconcile their cards and ledgers
with the results of the physical count.

3.4 Thus, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the BAC
Secretariat to regularly furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of the
POs, together with its supporting documents, within five (5) days upon
perfection thereof, for evaluation.

4.4 In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended that the
Honorable Mayor instruct the following:
a) Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for
each transfer and for future transfers from NGAs, whether or not
maintained in separate bank accounts;
b) Make the necessary corresponding adjustments to correct the
ledgers, trial balance, and the financial statements; and
c) The immediate submission of all un-submitted reports, if any,
pertaining to the aforementioned projects to the Auditor’s Office.
d) Include in the monitoring report of PPAs the physical and
financial status/accomplishment of the project.

5.4 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the
concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-
closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, and necessary supporting
schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the
end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

6.5 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
a) The immediate submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRM Funds for the months of May to December 2021;
b) On-time preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of und
LDRRMF by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis, duly noted by the
MDRRM Officer, and regular submission thereof to the Auditor’s Office on
or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
c) We also recommended that the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM Officer, be supported with
documentation of the programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but
not limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan,
copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization of QRF and
Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and photographs of
projects/programs/activities undertaken during the month of report.

7.4 We recommended and the Honorable Mayor concurred to enjoin the
Office of the Treasurer to:
a) Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled
checks; and
b) Prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month,
attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same
along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.

8.7 In view thereof, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct
the GAD Focal Person to:
a) Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report CY 2021 based on the
format prescribed in PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-
01 and submit the same to the Auditor’s Office immediately.
b) Henceforth, faithfully adhere to the timely submission of the GAD
AR, together with the GAD Plan and Budget, within five (5)
working days from the end of January of the preceding year; and
c) Regularly submit the quarterly report on GAD utilization to the
Auditor’s Office within the prescribed period of the Commission.

9.7 In view thereof, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct
the MPDO to:
a) Faithfully submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-
going PPAs and those to be implemented, with proper details as
mentioned in COA Circular 2013-004; and
b) Henceforth, prepare and regularly submit the monthly
monitoring report on the status of PPAs and quarterly report on
the utilization of 20% Development Fund to the Auditor’s Office.

11.5 We recommended and Management concurred that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer shall observe the stamping of “PAID”,
with the prescribed size of 2’’ x 3’’, on all the disbursement
vouchers and its supporting documents after payment is made.
b) The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment
shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents
to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in
payment of future claims.
c) The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including
its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s
Office are stamped “PAID”.

35)The Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three (3) priority programs and projects such as socio development, economic development and environment management pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

39)Faithfully impose on the municipal treasurer the daily preparation of the RCDs, duly supported by the necessary documents such as copies of the official receipts and validated deposit slips.

43) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to address and comply with the deficiencies noted during the validation and submit an updated copy of the same to the Auditor for review;
44)Moreover, require the same to strictly comply with the recording, updating, and maintaining of monthly accounting records, onwards; and

46)The Management shall direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
47)Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and
48)Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment of its constituents.
49)The Management shall furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.

50)The Management shall ensure that the Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly the BRS together with the original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in bank account;

51)Instruct the Municipal Accountant maintain a General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time; and
52)Require the inventory of the accountable forms for official receipts, prepare its RAAF and submit it immediately to the Auditor.

53)We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in its prescribed form.

4. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor mandate the concerned officials to furnish the audit team a copy of the duly approved Annual AIP pursuant to the provisions cited above, and require the same, every January of the ensuing years.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a) Adhere strictly with the regulation to ensure effective performance on the task assigned;

b) Submit monthly report of collections and report of accountable forms; and

c) Safely keep and turnover the used and issued accountable forms in sequence.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:

i. Designation of the accountable officer by the Head of the Agency or his duly authorized representative indicating the maximum accountability and purpose of cash advance (for initial cash advance);

ii. Certification from the Accountant that previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books;

iii. Approved application for bond for initial cash advance, and Fidelity Bond for the year for subsequent cash advance as the bond is renewable yearly; and

iv. Approved Budget Estimates and Approved Program of Activities.

v. Liquidation Vouchers and its supporting documents.

c) Direct the Municipal Budget officer, Municipal Accountant-Designate, and Municipal Treasurer who certifies to the existence of appropriation, the completeness of supporting documents and the availability of funds respectively and to explain in writing why the abovementioned cash advances was granted.

d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue letter of demand for those with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year.

c) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to: 

i. Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to institute a system such as E-Tracs that could facilitate the prompt determination, assessment, and collection of real property tax.

ii. Develop a program to encourage prompt settlement of tax obligation, such as tapping the services of the barangay officials in the locality where the real property is located.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Local Chief Executive:
a) Immediately constitute the Inventory Committee which shall
conduct the extensive and detailed physical count of all PPEs in
accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 and prepare the
RPCPPE thereon;
b) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously
post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and
property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep
and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the
Manual; and
c) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR,
and ARE as prescribed.

Therefore, we highly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate tracing of the initial recognition of the all
the PPE assets and compute the depreciation thereof based on
the latest estimated useful life of assets provided by the
Commission;
b) Instruct the Accountant to take up corresponding prior years’
adjustment in the books to be closed to the Government Equity
account; and
c) Instruct further the Accountant to disclose in the notes to
financial statements the useful lives of each category of
recognized PPE.
d) Henceforth, ensure that the Accountant religiously maintain the
pertinent ledgers and recognize depreciation expense on all PPE
accounts.

Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) cause
the immediate submission of both the approved supplemental budget
and supplemental AIP to the audit team to facilitate assessment on the
validity and appropriateness of PPAs implemented.

In view of the noted deficiencies, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee:
a) To submit certified true copies of certifications of posting
requirements by the BAC Secretariat relative to the BGCM PPA
procurements;
b) To immediately submit the lacking monthly reports on the
BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs;
c) Though late, still post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s
website or Facebook page and in conspicuous public places in
the locality for general transparency; and
d) Finally, furnish the audit team a copy of the written notice when
posting has been complied, including photos to validate posting
in conspicuous places in the locality.

Due to the foregoing lapses noted, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of
supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same
as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the
appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards;
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis; and
d) Direct the Inventory Committee to validate the same by
conducting a semestral physical count of inventory by items.
Thereafter, prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team not later than July 31, and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively.

Hence, we strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of the copy of the approved
Local Sanggunian Resolution on the release and utilization of the
QRF to the audit team;
b) Strictly instruct that the LDRRMO to religiously keep, maintain,
and update the three (3) RAAOs in accordance with the rules of
the Commission; and
c) Instruct the Accountant to draw appropriate journal entries to
record or reclassify the equipment pertaining to DRRM, if any.

Henceforth, we recommended that:
a) The Treasurer keep a permanent record book to keep track of
the receipt and issuances of accountable forms, and update the
same as needed;
b) The Treasurer conduct a regular month-end inventory of the
accountable forms, both with and without money value, to verify
existence, and reconcile the same with the permanent record
book; and
c) Prepare the monthly C/RAAFs at the end of each month,
complete and in accordance with the prescribed rules and
regulations.

Therefore, we recommended that the Treasurer, henceforth:
a) Attach all the necessary supporting documents such as duplicate
copies of ORs issued, cancelled, and voided, and validated
deposit slips to the RCDs; and
b) Prepare a schedule of cancelled/voided checks, if any, at the end
of the month, and attach the same to the RCD to be submitted to
the Auditor.

Therefore, steadfast, we recommended that:
a) The Accountant faithfully inspect and review records whether
the previous cash advances have been liquidated and properly
accounted for prior to the granting of the succeeding cash
advance;
b) The Accountant, after verification that are no cash advances
pending liquidation, issue a certification thereof prior to the
granting of succeeding cash advance; and
c) The Treasurer religiously liquidate the cash advance within the
prescribed period and in accordance with the rules and
regulations provided by the Commission.

In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended that the
Treasurer from now on conscientiously:
a) Record the transactions on a daily basis and stop the
practice of recording transactions based from the bank
statements;
b) Record the check issuances in a chronological order,
including the cancelled ones; and
c) Completely report and include in the RCD all collections
recorded in the cashbook.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Appoint a Certified Public Accountant as Municipal Accountant as required by law and in order that transactions of the Municipality will be correctly recorded.   If possible, augment personnel with accounting background in the Accounting Office that has the knowledge to prepare the reports needed for submission.
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate:
to submit copies of cancelled or paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos, and other documents necessary to facilitate the timely verification of the bank reconciliation statements.
to attend seminars or training related to preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and other reports related to the function of accountant.
to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in adherence to COA Circular No. 96-011. Also require her to get the monthly bank statements from the depository bank promptly and regularly.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e-access in the Land Bank of the Philippines and grant authority to the Municipal Accountant to access and view the balances on-line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Create an inventory appraisal/committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the General Services Officer.
If possible, designate a General Services Officer other than the Municipal Treasurer in order to focus on the PPE account because it is a huge account composing of 87% of the total assets.
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to:
Make necessary adjustments on the records for deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers;
After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment and should bear a Property Number; and
Provide residual value in computing the depreciation.
For check and balance, direct the General Service Office and Supply Office/Unit to maintain the equivalent Property Card (PC) for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of the same.
Direct the Administrative Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the same to GSIS. This will guarantee that all properties are adequately covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. For lost / damaged properties without covering insurance, liability therefore should devolve upon the parties responsible.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts;
Require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the depreciation policies under the Government Accounting Manual on LGUs. At the end of the useful life of the property, the agency should provide the residual value equivalent to five per cent of the PPEs’ acquisition cost; and

We further recommended that henceforth, management should conform to the policies prescribed in CSC MC No. 01, s, 2001 in the granting of awards that:

•	at least 5% of the HRD Funds shall be allocated for the PRAISE and incorporated in the Agency’s annual work and financial plan and budget.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

Inasmuch as the PRAISE Committee recommended and subsequently approved by the Sangguniang Bayan the grant of Personnel Motivation Incentive to LGU officials and employees in consideration of their very satisfactory rating for the year, it was also clearly provided in the CSC Memorandum that in the PRAISE System, monetary awards shall be granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts result in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20% of the savings generated and a definite selection procedure be followed by the Committee.

Moreover, an SB Ordinance cannot be used as appropriate legal basis in granting personal benefits pursuant to Section 4 (1.) of P.D. 1445 in that these ordinances are not laws by themselves and that their passage does not qualify as an appropriate specific authority of law. In several lines of opinions and decided  cases, the Supreme Court clarified that the phrase “specific authority of law” refers to enactments by the Congress itself, or issuance from the Office of the President, either directly or through the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), which is its principal arm tasked with matters pertaining to compensation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

Direct the Municipal General Service Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to take appropriate action to facilitate the land titling were the Municipal Hall, office building, health center, and basketball court are situated in favor of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements and furnish COA the corrections made therein.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management:
Enforce the immediate submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports and prepare demand letters to be issued to accountable officers for cash advances that are due and demandable;
Deduct from salaries of accountable officers who are still connected with the LGU whose cash advances are outstanding beyond the prescribed period; and
Send final demand letters to persons who have retired or out of service that still have unliquidated cash advances; else, undertake appropriate legal actions for the recovery/ settlement of the unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
Outstanding balances be deducted from salaries of concerned accountable officers who are still connected with the LGU, as previously agreed by the management in our prior recommendations;
The Municipal Accountant initiate the conduct of verification, analysis and validation of dormant accounts and follow the procedures and guidelines in the preparation of documents for the filing of request for write-off, where applicable.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Retrieve the documents affecting the accounts to determine the accounting entries causing the abnormal balances of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts in the subsidiary ledgers; and rectify the errors by preparing the JEV of the adjusting entry, copy furnish COA;
Conduct regular monitoring of the individual (subsidiary ledger) accounts composing the trial balance (general ledger) accounts logged by the accounting staffs before certifying the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant dig up the source documents or relevant transactions pertaining to the liabilities recorded in the subsidiary ledgers and determine liabilities that need to be settled by preparation of appropriate adjusting entries or return of remaining fund to source agencies; furnish COA with the JEV taking up the adjusting/correcting entries relative to the accounts Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities.
We further recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant maintain records and source documents necessary for the proper monitoring and timely settlement of liabilities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the journal entries recorded for each of the identified transactions and record appropriate adjustments/corrections in accordance with IPSAS 17 for the fair presentation of balances in the financial statements, copy furnish COA of the entries made.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry to reclassify completed PPE still included in the Construction in Progress account to the proper PPE accounts; and recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation of all PPE in accordance with IPSAS 17 to fairly present their balances in the financial statements, copy furnish COA of the entries made.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Require the GSO/Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all local roads and a road map alongside the preparation of the Annual report of Local Road Network;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to establish the necessary records and provide adjusting journal entries to recognize the changes and depreciation expenses/impairment losses upon completion of the required inventory reports in compliance to COA Circular 2015-008 for the proper recognition and subsequent measurement of local roads and its components.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
Physical count of inventory, property, plant and equipment be conducted  and the required reports be completed, reconciled and submitted to COA for our validation;
The Accounting and General Services Offices adopt systems and procedures as required in NGAS Manual for the proper accounting and recording of Inventories and PPEs, maintaining the necessary ledger cards and schedules for substantiation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management immediately contact MDFO for clarification of the schedule of the exact amount of loan amortizations to be strictly monitored and settled on or before due dates to avoid penalties which shall be personally chargeable against the liable official/employee.
We also recommended that Management provide justification with supporting documents that we will evaluate to validate the initial explanation provided by the Municipal Treasurer as penalties and surcharges are considered as unnecessary expenditures defined in Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and not chargeable against the 20% Development Fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management:
Expedite the execution of the planned projects and activities under the 20% DF from CY 2015-2019 and strictly observe proper timelines, install effective monitoring of the fund utilization and implementation in compliance to Section 5 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1;
Carefully review and evaluate the balances of appropriation of the continuing fund from CY 2015-2018 and recommend for approval the reversion of unexpended balances which are no longer needed in connection with the activities funded by the 20% DF subject to the provisions of Section 322 of RA 7160 and other prevailing applicable laws and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to establish the nature/class of the identified expenditures for proper realignment of charging if the nature of expenses is for regular repairs and maintenance or should be treated as capitalizable costs to be added to their corresponding asset accounts, copy furnish COA of the appropriate adjusting entries made.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality completely man the LDRRMO pursuant to Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of R.A. No. 10121, that the office be composed of three DRRM staffs exclusively responsible for (1) administration and training; (2) research and planning; (3) operations and warning, strictly observing the minimum standards and qualifications prescribed in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management:
Require the LDRRM Officer to accomplish the annual LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in order to provide the necessary information for the preparation of the reports and proper utilization and implementation of projects/activities;
Require the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO staff concerned to prepare the monthly and annual reports using the prescribed formats and establish the correct balances to be recorded; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in the transfer of the correct balance to the special trust fund account.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the journal entries recorded for each of the identified transactions in Appendix G and make necessary adjustments/corrections in compliance to NDRRMC, DBM & DILG JMC Circular No. 2013-1 and for the fair presentation of balances in the financial statements in accordance with COA Circular 2016-006, copy furnish COA of the entries made.
We further recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to carefully examine each disbursement for proper recognition and recording of accounts and regularly coordinate with the LDRRMO pertaining to the monitoring of the actual dispense of inventories/materials purchased.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management provide justification on the necessity of the application of the alternative mode of procurement for the said disbursements which should fall under the conditions set forth in the IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended that henceforth, procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding, unless the conditions to resort to alternative modes of procurement be justified and met in order to avail the most advantageous prices of the goods to be procured.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management submit to COA the following for review and evaluation:
Copy of the LGU Employee Suggestions and Incentive Awards System (PRAISE) duly approved by the Civil Service Commission
Agency annual work and financial plan and budget showing that at least 5% of the HRD Funds was allocated for the PRAISE
Copy of the annual report on the awards and incentives system which should be submitted to the CSC on or before the 30th day of January; and
Justification/explanation on the procedures in evaluating the nominees and the mechanism for recognizing the awardees.
We further recommended that henceforth, management should conform with the policies prescribed in CSC MC No. 01, s, 2001 in the granting of awards that:
monetary awards shall be granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts result in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20% of the savings generated; and
at least 5% of the HRD Funds shall be allocated for the PRAISE and incorporated in the Agency’s annual work and financial plan and budget.
payments be supported with documents proving that the recipients  met the conditions set forth in CSC MC No. 01, s, 2001.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management submit:
Certified photocopies of employees’ leave cards as at last date of service duly audited by the Personnel Division and COA/ Certificate of leave credits issued by the Admin/Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) and the Computation of terminal leave benefits duly signed/certified by the accountant for all of the identified retired/resigned/deceased LGU officials and employees to present the detailed computation of the payments made; and
All the unsubmitted supporting documents individually identified in Appendix I in compliance to COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management revisit the payroll deductions and strictly comply with the minimum net take-home pay of ₱5,000 provided under Section 52 of RA 11260. Any deficiency in loan payments and other obligations as a result of the mandatory minimum requirement may be addressed through direct payment arrangements to be voluntarily made by the employees to the concerned institutions.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the concerned departments purchasing the inventories and other supplies held for distribution for the initial recording and monitoring of the actual distribution of said inventories and supplies.
We also recommended that the target beneficiaries/recipients be identified and that the planned programs be carefully administered for the effective implementation of projects for the attainment of the desired goals.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management immediately submit the required supporting documents for medical assistance as enumerated in the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 2019-11 for our review and validation.
We also recommended that financial assistance under the AICS program be strictly administered by the social workers for the proper implementation of the guidelines and equitable distribution of assistance to clients in crisis situations.

Recommendation:
Hence, for effective and equitable distribution of financial assistance and to establish the regularity, validity and propriety of the grants, we recommended that LGU Tagoloan strictly follow the applicable guidelines formulated by the DSWD and require sufficient supporting documents in the grant of financial assistance to the constituents.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management:
Create/Strengthen the LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and the Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) system to carry out the provisions and mandates of R.A. No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, observing the correct composition of the GFPS;
Ensure that all members of the GFPS and M&E undergo capacity building programs on GAD which shall include but not be limited to: gender sensitivity training (GST), gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF), Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba (GeRL Ka Ba) Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) and other succeeding GAD tools which shall be chargeable to GAD Fund;
Administer the HGDG in the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget to be submitted to the Provincial Government for consolidation and submission to the DILG Regional Office, coordinating with the DILG municipal LGOO for assistance; and
Incorporate the HGDG in the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report together with the required supporting documents.
We also recommended that management carefully review and observe the detailed guidelines provided under the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the process of the institutionalizing effective gender mainstreaming in LGU Tagoloan.

1. We recommended that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three (3) priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

2. We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and submit Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDDRM Council.

3. We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in compliance with the above quoted rules.
5. The Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.

6. We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
7. Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
8. Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment of its constituents.
9. We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.

10. We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly the BRS together with the original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in bank account.

11. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time.
12. We further recommended that Management require the inventory of the accountable forms for official receipts, prepare its RAAF and submit it immediately to the Auditor

13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in its prescribed form.

14. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the law in the preparation and submission of Reports of Collections and Deposits and its supporting documents like the Official Receipts and Validated Deposit Slips on a monthly basis.

15. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.
16. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the Auditor.
17. Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor and the prompt submission thereafter.

18. We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

• Fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Section 443 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, for more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.

• Give priority and immediately hire a full pledged Municipal Accountant, a CPA to occupy the mandatory position of the LGU. Advertise the immediate hiring of a CPA for the position of the Municipal Accountant giving priority residents of the municipality. 

• Submit explanation/justification of the deferment / non-hiring of a CPA which is a mandatory position of the LGU of which is attributed to the breakdown on internal control and the failure to submit accurate/complete reports for the year. 

• Direct the Municipal Human Resource Officer to publish the vacancy and if no willing and qualified applicant, recommend for the temporary appointment of the available applicant.
observations.managementAction: No CPA was hired for the position of Municipal Accountant and other vacant positions were not filled out.
observations.reason: No applicants for the vacant positions

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that Management widen the allocation of
utilization of 20% development fund to the three (3) priority
programs and projects such as social development, economic
development and environment management pursuant to DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017.

3.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a) The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete
supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended
LDRRM Fund in the amount of P712,000.00 from General Fund
to Special Trust Fund, and.
b) The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended
LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the
appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.

i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of
Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds
(Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in
Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-
01-02-010).
ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank
– Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-
010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-
020).

3.5 We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and
other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts
and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its
perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents
pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

4.6 We recommended that Management
a) Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and
b) Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological
Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of
public health and environment of its constituents.

5.3 We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of
the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects
and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend
that for the CY 2019, the GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared
and submitted immediately to the Audit Team.

7.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in
its prescribed form.

8.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer
to strictly comply with the provisions of the law in the preparation
and submission of Reports of Collections and Deposits and its
supporting documents like the Official Receipts and Validated Deposit
Slips on a monthly basis.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of
Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together
with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of
the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the
NGAS for LGUs.
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and
evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then
prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the
Auditor.
c) Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate
submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor and the
prompt submission thereafter.

We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the work assignments of the accounting staff to ensure that accounting policies and procedures are adhered to such as compliance with Section 111(1) of PD 1445 and Section 4(e) of the NGAS for LGUs; and see to it that required journals and ledgers are complete before certifying the Financial Statements.
We further recommended that management maintain updated Subsidiary Ledgers that could easily be traced to sources of information/references and corroborate with the General Ledger accounts to ascertain the existence, validity and accuracy of the reported balances.

We therefore recommended that Management:
Enjoin the Accountant to maintain SLs for all fund transfers for the proper monitoring of the Due to NGAs account, and trace back prior years’ balances and sources of information;
Closely monitor the implementation of each fund transfer and liquidate or return unutilized balances; and
Obtain a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement covering the fund transfers and see that terms and conditions are complied as stipulated in the contract.

We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the work assignments of the accounting staff to ensure that accounting policies and procedures are adhered to such as compliance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS for LGUs and Section 111(1) of PD 1445; and see to it that required journals and ledgers are complete before certifying Financial Statements.
We further recommended that management maintain updated Subsidiary Ledgers that could easily be traced to sources of information/references and corroborate with the General Ledger accounts to ascertain the existence, validity and accuracy of the reported balances.

We recommended that management enjoin the accounting department and LDRRMO to: (a) observe proper recognition of the purchased supplies and inventories in the asset and expense accounts; and (b) regularly reconcile the records with the actual inventories in compliance to NDRRMC, DBM & DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1,  for the fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer implement the remedies set forth in Article 347 of A.O. No. 270 by administrative or judicial actions which are (a) levy on real property, and (b) sale of real property at public auction after the procedures prescribed in A.O. No. 270 are already undertaken; and
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile their balances for the proper reporting and fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit immediately the still unsubmitted paid vouchers, reports and supporting documents for the periods indicated for all funds of the Municipality;
Strictly observe the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No. 2009-006 in the timely and complete submission of accounts and reports; and
Coordinate with the employees concerned in the preparation of documents and install necessary measures for the complete submission and prompt consolidation of vouchers.

We recommended that Management:
Submit to COA justification and legal basis, if any, for the release of the cash gift prior to the prescribed schedule of release; and
Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions and guidelines provided by the authorized agencies in the release of bonuses and similar disbursements to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenses which are subject to suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that management submit a thorough explanation and justification on the selection process and granting of the Personal Motivation Incentives to officials and employees and the legal basis, if any, in the funding source/appropriation of the said disbursement.
We further recommended that henceforth, management should conform to the policies prescribed in CSC MC No. 01, s, 2001 in the granting of awards that:
monetary awards shall be granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts result in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20% of the savings generated; and
at least 5% of the HRD Funds shall be allocated for the PRAISE and incorporated in the Agency’s annual work and financial plan and budget.

We therefore recommended that management:
Furnish COA a copy of the annual "Report on Overtime Services With Pay" submitted to DBM and CSC pursuant to paragraph no. 13.1 of  CSC-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2015; and
Henceforth, submit complete documentary requirements relative to the overtime payments as enumerated in Section 5.10 of COA Circular 2012-001 whenever overtime services are deemed necessary.

We therefore recommended that Management:
Provide the legal basis on the grant of Representation Allowance to said personnel and furnish COA copies of the authority to collect RA or its equivalent;
Make necessary adjustments in the Journal Entries erroneously recorded to effect correction of the recognized expense; and
Stop using RERs as supporting documents to the RA claims and observe the prescribed documentary requirements provided in COA Circular 2012-002 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 286, Article 1, Chapter 5, Volume 1 of the GAAM.

We therefore recommended that management:
Observe the recommended time schedules for the respective procurement activity; and
Closely monitor the completion of the required supporting documents to facilitate the proper procedures of the procurement to avoid the delay in the implementation of projects and such other regular operations of the government.

We recommended that henceforth, management instruct the responsible departments to furnish COA the POs and all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five (5) working days from execution of said POs pursuant to item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that henceforth, management require the GSO to comply with the provisions of Section 376 of RA 7160 in the procurement of drugs and medicine by
Covering the procurement with written contract or Purchase Order;
Accomplishing the Inspection and Acceptance Report at once upon delivery of the items procured; and
Recording the delivered items in the appropriate stock/property cards exercising due diligence in the monitoring, utilization and safekeeping thereof, to deter the incurrence of loss or theft and to be able to efficiently deliver the drugs and medicines to the appropriate and intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the accounting department observe proper recognition of the purchased drugs and medicines in the asset and expense accounts and regularly reconcile the records with the actual inventories for the fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

We therefore recommended that Management:
Enjoin the Accounting, GSO and Engineering Departments to gather the needed data to establish the required reports provided in COA Circular 2015-008 for the proper recognition and subsequent measurement of local roads and its components; and
Review the outstanding CIP-Infrastructure Assets account and transfer any completed Public Infrastructure to the appropriate asset accounts and maintain subsidiary ledgers for proper monitoring.

We recommended that henceforth, LGU-Tagoloan require the recipients of the cash assistance to support their claims with the necessary and appropriate documentation depending on the nature of transactions as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
We also recommended that management explain/justify the inconsistencies noted in the identified disbursement vouchers in Appendix A-12.

We recommended that the officials concerned prepare Driver’s Trip Tickets duly filled up with the respective plate numbers of vehicles charging their fuel consumption against LGU-Tagoloan to ensure the proper utilization of government motor transportation and fuel consumption.
We recommended that management deduct from salaries of the concerned accountable officers the balances of their outstanding cash advances.
We recommended that management deduct from salaries of concerned accountable officers the balances of their outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers acting as custodians of local funds of LGU-Tagoloan be adequately bonded and compliant with the amounts of fidelity bonds set forth in Annex C of Treasury Circular no. 02-2009 dated August 06, 2009, in pursuance of Section 305(f) of Republic Act No. 7160, to safeguard government funds.

We recommended that management direct pertinent officials to prepare and submit the consolidated Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of all the insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and COA Circular 2018-002 in order to indemnify or compensate the government for any damage to, or loss of, its properties due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other casualty that may arise.
We further recommended that management maintain updated Subsidiary Ledgers that could easily be traced to sources of information/references and corroborate with the General Ledger accounts to ascertain the existence and accuracy of the reported balances and to properly calculate the corresponding depreciation and other valuations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Honorable Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should exert more efforts in enforcing collection on the Real Property Taxes since no payment has been made despite several demand letters were mailed to every barangay.
